title: thymus serpyllum var. arenarius
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. arenarius, also known as the sea thyme or coastal thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. It is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with tiny pink or white flowers, valued for its medicinal and culinary properties. The leaves and flowers of Thymus serpyllum var. arenarius contain antimicrobial and antifungal compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin problems like acne and wounds. In herbal preparations, sea thyme is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to promote respiratory health, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system.
